[Updated 08/01/2025]

I made the decision to use Michele's services again thinking that based on the bad initial experience followed by her coming through things would be alright. Unfortunately, Michele has yet again left me with weeks of unanswered emails, is intentionally not taking my calls or responding to my messages.

She let me know earlier on in my case that she required my partner to have separate representation which we paid for out of pocket, and unfortunately seems to have been an additional waste of money because Michele is unresponsive to my partner's attorney as well.

This is truly unacceptable for any service provider let alone an attorney who is supposed to be acting in your best interest. I have weeks of emails unanswered, and when she finally did pick up the phone and confirmed that she would have my document ready by the end of the day - that was also a lie. Not only is it not ready she is continuing to avoid communication at all.

I've been nothing but accommodating and respectful in all of my communication and the bare minimum expectation is to get the service I pay for which is absolutely not what you will get with Michele. I highly recommend you avoid this attorney and her office because odds are it is going to cost you way more anxiety and stress than it's worth.

I genuinely wish I had engaged another attorney and deeply regret working with Michele.
_______________________________________________________________

[Initial review]

Maybe one of the worst experiences I've had with any service I've had in WA. I contacted Michele in June, 2023, and had a phone appointment scheduled with her on 6/30/23 to talk to her about my situation. We had what I thought was a great discussion about why I was looking to engage her services as an attorney, and she assured me that I would receive an email follow-up from her that day with the documents she talked to me about in our conversation.

It was 4th of July weekend following that conversation, so when I didn't receive a follow-up I gave her the leverage that it was a Friday leading up to a long weekend and everyone deserves that kind of leeway. By 7/14 I still hadn't heard from her, and sent a follow-up email, and then another, and then another with no response.

I was in Europe on a business trip and called her from there and was finally able to get her over the phone near 7/20 when she apologized for not responding and claimed it was because she had recently taken on a number of additional clients but confirmed that it was not an excuse for me not being taken seriously and that I was still important to her. She confirmed on that day that I would receive an email with the documents by the end of that day. Which never happened.

On 7/25 I emailed her letting her know this was totally unacceptable and I received a response letting me know we could schedule a phone call the next morning, which I didn't need to do. I let her know that I didn't need a phone call, I needed the documents she had told me on multiple occasions that I would receive multiple times before. I never received the documents. She has not responded to emails, phone calls, or text messages since.

Michele is super unpredictable. She will come off as a trustworthy attorney over the phone, but will never follow up, keep dragging you along with the hope that she's going to follow up, and then never will. I'm desperate for a new attorney now with being a month late on my legal case which should and could have been handled within that first week.

Avoid.

Michele Haaseth from Haaseth Law recently worked with us on our estate plan, will, and health directives. Her work was stellar, service excellent, and she is a very enjoyable person to work with. I highly recommend Haaseth Law for any of your legal needs.
Periodically ghosted, so timeline increases dramatically

Michele assisted my family in a name change matter. She was able to explain the process from start to finish and explain any fees we could expect to pay. She is quick to respond to emails and I would use her services again, if needed. Overall awesome attorney to work with!

I hired Michele to work on a Personal Injury matter. She was smart, skilled and knowledgeable. She gave me a timeline of how things would work, and let me know what the next steps were. I really appreciated her help with my matter and was very happy with the outcome.  I highly recomend Michele!

I cannot believe Michelle is still in business! I reached out back in 2017 for assistance with a cohabitation agreement. She came recommended by other lawyers working in the same building. After an initial phone call and sending her some paperwork, she GHOSTED me on our next scheduled call and stopped responding to all email. She did this AFTER  I sent her all my info and paperwork. Incredibly unprofessional and a total scam. I thought she had maybe gone out of business or something but no, I see that she is still in operation. Avoid working with her at all costs.

We were very pleased with Michele Hasseth as our attorney.  She was dependable, responsive, knowledgable and handled all of our needs in a timely manner.  We recommend her services without reservation.

Michele came across as very responsive and provided verbal commitment to work with us. We sent all the requested details and followed up several times over email and phone but never heard back from her. It would have been useful to know that she had decided to not take us on as a client.

Michele has been entirely unresponsive since I began working with her earlier this year. The relationship started very professional with updates but has come to a point where no work has actually been delivered (I just needed a letter of demand written and sent) and she never responded to my last voicemail and email. I am not sure if there is something going on with her personally but I at minimum expected a heads up she couldn’t manage my case anymore and/or a referral to a colleague. Im really disappointed by the amount of time I’ve lost on this because of her.

Attorney Haaseth stepped in to assist with the legal documents that I needed to submit to the court for a guardianship. She made time for me, was cordial and very knowledgable. I highly recommend her for your legal needs.

Reached out to Aaron's office for liability advice and insurance claim help. Communication was terrible, he did not remember the purpose of reaching out and never followed up with my insurance company to see what was happening. There was no urgency to speak with me after several requests and promises. I have a long email thread asking for updates and him telling me that he had not heard anything from the insurance and to be patient, but he'd reach out to check. I eventually found out my claim had been denied in the first month so there was no way he had actually been calling to check on it for the last 3 months or he would have known this. When I finally received a call back, he said it was my fault the claim was denied, and getting the claim accepted was not a reason I reached out to him. He then accused me of changing my story (the reason for hiring him) because it did not match up with his notes of him supposed to be helping me with the claim. When I tried to explain that there clearly must have been a miscommunication and wanted to clarify my reason for reaching out, he said he did not care he no longer wanted to represent me after wasting 3 months of my time.

Subject: Unjust Legal Pursuit of a Small Veteran-Owned Business

As a Gulf War veteran, I've strived to uphold the values of integrity and service in all my endeavors. It is, therefore, profoundly disheartening to find my small business, which receives a mere 110 website visitors per month, targeted by Higbee & Associates. After inadvertently displaying an Associated Press (AP) image on our website for a short period, we promptly removed it upon notification. Despite this immediate compliance, Higbee & Associates has chosen to pursue aggressive legal action, demanding fees that are grossly disproportionate to any conceivable damages.​

Given our minimal web traffic and consistent financial losses, the assertion that AP suffered significant harm from this limited exposure is, in my opinion, unfounded. It appears that Higbee & Associates employs these tactics to exploit small businesses, leveraging intimidation to extract settlements irrespective of actual impact.​

This ordeal has been both financially and emotionally taxing, especially as we navigate the challenges of bankruptcy. I urge others to remain vigilant and seek legal counsel when confronted by such practices, which I perceive to be exploitative.

reviews.text:
I hired Kris to help me get full custody of my children. I was just about 1-year into a divorce which included a parenting plan requiring 50/50 custody with my ex and a child support order that had me paying each month. My ex had essentially abandoned the children and her obligation but I was stuck with the court orders, thus stuck with paying child support and not having any closure for the children. It was an incredibly stressful situation and Kris was on top of things from day 1. I could write a 10-page review about all that went on but I’ll summarize by saying he achieved the outcome I was looking for. Furthermore, he secured temporary orders, almost immediately after taking my case, which provided my family with a tremendous amount of relief during the rest of the court proceedings.
Prior to meeting with Kris I had talked with several other attorneys. I had a lot of legal experience having handled my own divorce pro se. I felt like most were just telling me what I wanted to hear and didn’t seem to care much about me. In a few cases I thought I would be better off representing myself. Kris was different. I hired him on the spot.
He listened, he understood, and he explained things very clearly. He does not provide false hope. He explains strategies clearly and allows you to control the level of risk/reward you are comfortable taking. In my case he delivered exactly what we had talked about in our very first meeting. I felt confident in Kris’s representation. That’s huge when you’re going through any type of stressful case. I never worried that “the other team” would somehow get one over on him. They never did.
There’s an office behind Kris and I’d be remiss if I didn’t mention some of the other things that made my experience less stressful. Kris utilizes a paralegal to handle legal filings, documentation updates, and other activities around a legal case. Melodie was assigned to my case and she was outstanding. Our filings were always on time or early, our responses were timely, and I never worried about missing a deadline or having anything fall through the cracks. Shay in the billing department was also very pleasant. Bills were clear and easy to understand. Bills arrive via email and so do receipts for payment. This made it simple to review, pay, and track my activity from my phone. The office itself is pleasant and peaceful and it feels very successful. I wouldn’t know if they have a voice mail system as my calls were always answered by a human and returned same day if Kris wasn’t available. Emails were also returned same day or early the next day if I sent one off in the late evening.
Kris will definitely be my attorney should I ever require legal assistance in the future. I’m certain he could “duke it out in court” but his demeanor and professionalism secured my outcome without having to go there. He’s always focused on securing the outcome and he works very well with opposing council to achieve it.
The staff is most pleasant, professional, and knowledgeable.   They were able to accommodate me during this COVID-19 pandemic. Many Thanks !!!

I was at an roadblock in my dealings with my ex. The kids were being brought into adult situations. As a result, I had reached out to several attorneys for help. They had all really just didnt want the hassle of my case. Kris listened to me and took excellent notes. He gave me options that would help alleviate the stress on me. Subsequently, he and his team reviewed the merits of the case and determined the best course of action to resolve the continued cycle of stress and conflict.

In the proceeding, Kris was calm and susinct in his approach which ultimately saved me time and money. We won the motion and he able to give me and my family the peace that we needed.

Alienation is never a good thing but Kris and the team at O'Brian work together to bring a viable solution to reality. They were open to looking for options that would solve the issues and bring peace to conflict.

I had to search for a last minute lawyer when my marriage for 14 years was over. Not only did this office take on my case but I was able to come in that day to speak with someone. My lawyers were always prepared and helpful through my whole process. The complete staff was amazing and understanding.

Catherine Victorino & her paralegal, Ami, are amazing! They helped me throughout the whole process and they were super informative and supportive through it all. I can honestly say that they both genuinely cared about my case and did everything in their power to make sure that the outcome was the best possible for my child and myself. We just recently were able to wrap up my case by getting my ex to settle and avoided trial. Couldn’t be happier

Catherine Victornio is a very proficient attorney, but she has genuine empathy for her clients.  In my opinion, this makes her push herself harder to get the desired outcome.  This is exactly what she did for me, including following up after the case had concluded to check that I was doing well.
From my initial contact with Cate, it was obvious that she was already well versed in everything it was going to take to get my divorce taken care of.  To say she was conscientious and caring would be an understatement.
We had to work together long distance, and her whole staff worked collectively to make certain all documents, signatures, and anything necessary was processed accurately and timely.  Every question I asked, they already had answered.  My entire experience with the team felt more like they were friends or family who were taking care of me rather that a group of people I had not met shuffling papers.
Because of the tight time-frame we had to work together, I sent way too emails and documentation and called more that I should have.  Cate was always reassuring and managed to sift through mountains of documents to get just what the court needed.  Her tasks would have probably been simpler without my impediment, but she got through all that with grace, dignity and won.  Make a note of that – she won what I had been told was a losing battle.
Cate’s knowledge of the law and the legal system in general is second to none.  She easily recognized when the opposing side was up to no good and quickly walked away and strengthened our position.  She labored under difficult circumstances and a shortened time frame to achieve the very best outcome for me – exactly the outcome I needed.
Her expertise notwithstanding, it was her caring and understanding that really helped me get through this ordeal.  The entire team made me feel comforted, confident and assured.  I am aware of how many late nights Cate worked hard for me and worked hard to keep me at ease.  Cate continuously made me aware of what to expect and what the possibilities might be, I was never in the dark about the process.  It is most likely her compassion that has strengthened her abilities.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Jodi!  I agree - Cate has been a wonderful addition to our firm.  Not only is she a great attorney, she has good insight and is a true professional and a good person.

My first attorney, Kathryn Peterson, took my case even though it was clearly unlikely to be an easy or profitable one. She fought tooth and nail for me, and best of all, she won. She put out fires left and right, caused by my ex-husband, daughter's father, and my own emotional outbursts.

I was initially worried when Kathryn transferred my divorce case to another attorney in the firm, Catherine Victorino, but my fears were swiftly put to rest when Cate began plowing through the piles of paperwork and evidence (to alleviate concerns, my case is MASSIVE, with multiple moving parts; the transfer was motivated by time constraints and hostility on the part of opposing counsel).

It was clear from the get-go that her top priority was seeing me fairly treated and settled in court. She went to bat for me numerous times when opposing counsel stepped out of line and stone-walled negotiations completely.

This is the ONLY firm you will find on this side of Washington that cares more about their clients than cash; a frankly moving attribute to find today when even the exchange of a credit card for a milkshake at the drive-thru can feel like the end of a Mexican Stand-off.

While both of my cases are still working, I feel confident with the help of every single person in this office- seriously, the front desk staff actually bother to remember my name and Ami (paralegal) is a down-to-earth genius. Thank you all for your help!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Korista, for affirming what I already know.  The people in this firm truly care about their clients.  I do so appreciate your statement.

reviews.text:
Finding a probate litigation attorney felt nearly impossible. Those I managed to contact offered various reasons why they couldn’t help, often declining even a consultation. The referral from KCBA was simply “too busy to take anything on.”

Meanwhile, I’ve spent the last 15 months watching two-thirds of the estate beneficiaries act unethically at different intervals. Now, with the estate set to close, I’ve received a waiver to sign. The pain of witnessing “family” members turn into animals as they feast on the dead has been overwhelming—it’s left me feeling lost, heartbroken, and utterly alone. I find it hard to think straight. Is this really happening?

Determined to gain clarity, I organized my questions and outlined the backstory. I wanted to make the most of any free consultation and be respectful of the attorney’s time. After three days of searching, I finally scheduled a consultation with Nowakowski Legal PLLC.

My consultant was deeply empathetic, listening carefully to my concerns. They reassured me that probate laws are straightforward and encouraged me to request specific changes to the waiver. We reviewed each of my questions, and by the end of our conversation, I felt empowered to move forward on my own, confident that I didn’t need further legal assistance at this stage.

The insight and peace of mind I received were invaluable. I walked away wanting to show my gratitude—if I could, I’d buy my consultant lunch, but instead, I’m leaving this review. Nowakowski Legal PLLC is the real deal: they offer genuine guidance and truly helpful insight into your legal concerns.

reviews.text: This review is long overdue. I have a very high conflict, extremely litigious, abusive ex whom we share a very young child together that I needed to ensure theirs and mine’s safety. I had my initial consultation with Vincent and what he advised resonated with me on many levels. This guy knows his stuff! Joe originally represented me through my DVPO case against my ex and then my divorce. My ex fought me every step of the way with multiple motions in between, but Joe was there and fought hard and we prevailed each and every time. My ex on the other hand went through multiple attorneys eventually going pro se. My divorce inevitably went to trial. While there were some things I was disappointed in, it was a pretty good outcome for our child, which is all that mattered. I was awarded full legal and physical custody. My ex received visitations built in phases with multiple restrictions/requirements for mental illness, substance abuse, and DV. My DVPO was also extended an additional 3 years. Once the divorce/parenting plan was finalized, I thought the legal abuse would be done. My ex then proceeded to file multiple motions pro se ranging from contempt to emergency custody. This is where Page stepped in to represent me. It was overwhelming as I had multiple frivolous motions going on at the same time that hold serious implications if not responded to appropriately. Page was amazing! We also filed and obtained an Order for Abusive Litigation against my ex, and the previous motions filed by him were all dismissed as meritless. Divorce is not easy for anyone. It’s even harder with children involved. It has taken a huge emotional toll on me. However, Vincent and his team have always done right by me and my child. I feel they represented me in the way I wanted to be represented… with honesty and integrity. They told my voice in a way that was legally understood. They were always honest with me about the situation, extremely knowledgeable, very professional and most importantly they showed me empathy during a very difficult time. They provided me with a tremendous amount of peace of mind during this process. I cannot recommend Vincent and team enough. Even though the litigation itself was a horrendous experience, working with Vincent’s firm was overall amazing. I also need to add that Lindsey is an absolute gem, as well! It was always nice to chat with her when I called in. I was always disappointed if it was someone else filling in for her 😂

reviews.text:
I am a 53 year old female who was diagnosed with Young Onset Parkinson’s Disease in January 2023, four months later my husband left me and filed for a divorce.  This was beyond devastating to say the least.  I was in a bad spot and unable to pay for an attorney without going through a program in my state called Moderate Means.  This program allows attorneys to select cases they are willing to work on at a discounted rate.  Vincent Humphrey who is the managing attorney at this firm selected my case.  The first thing that I should have inquired about was the $ processing fee they charged me to take my retainer payment. This is how they used my money?

It got much, much worse.  They charged me to read emails I sent to them asking for help in obtaining tax documents that my husband was refusing to provide me with and was in contempt of court for refusing to provide.  I emailed at least 7 times asking for the documents I needed in order to itemize my return and was billed to read the emails but they did nothing, not even a response. The next big issue that took place was they asked me to provide them with all of my financials stuff when I first hired them to represent me and they did absolutely nothing with them.  Months later my husband filed a motion for Temporary Support and my attorney asked me to provide her the same information including records from the months that had passed since I first jumped through this hoop.  It gets worse.  At the hearing for Temporary Support, I find out my attorney did not properly upload my financial records to the court and the hearing has to be postponed.  I made sure to say to my attorney, I trust I am not going to be charged for the time to prep for this hearing or to attend it?  I hear nothing back but I am of course charged to read the email and charged for the time it took for them to screw up the hearing.

Long story short?  They did nothing for me and took $9500 from me. They are sitting on the balance of my retainer refusing to return it to me because I am disputing their billing.  To top it all off?  My Mom paid for my legal fees with the understanding that I would pay her back once I settle my divorce.  Mr. Humphrey went so far as to indicate that it should not matter to me that he is holding onto my money because my Mom paid him.

They are a horror!
reviews.ownerResponse: Amy, unfortunately given ethical considerations we can’t address your concerns on a public platform. However, each of your concerns have been addressed privately, and we truly hope that you find the advocacy that you are looking for.
